---
name: New Module
about: Propose a new curriculum module
title: '[MODULE] Track Name — Module Title'
labels: 'new-module'
---

## Track
Which track does this belong to? (00-foundations / 01-offensive / etc.)

## Module title
Short descriptive name.

## Learning objective
One sentence: what will the learner be able to do after completing this? (Measurable, job-relevant.)

## The core idea (the bridge)
A few sentences of the **original prose** this module will add — the mental model, the practitioner
translation ("X is really the Y you already know"), the synthesis or judgment no single existing
resource gives. Per the hybrid model (see `CLAUDE.md`), this is the part you *write*; the
step-by-step "how it works" you *curate* in **Learn**. If you can't name what original insight this
module adds, it's probably a link list, not a module.

## Learn (the curated path)
The best existing resources for the raw explanation — grouped, with a line on why each earns the
learner's time. (Specific links, not bare channels or 200-page manuals.)

## Lab
What does the learner build and commit? Is it a **reference lab** (ships a one-command
`docker-compose` + `data/` + `Makefile`) or does it point at an external vulnerable target
(DVWA / Vulhub / CloudGoat)?

## OSS tools used
Open-source first. Free/community editions are fine where that's what the job uses.

## Estimated time
How long should this take a learner?

## Your background
Optional: brief note on your experience with this topic.
